name: cfl-common
documentation: Common package for Code for Life

functions.name: common.utils._using_two_factor
documentation: Returns whether the user is using 2fa or not.

functions.name: common.utils.using_two_factor
documentation: Returns whether the user is using 2fa or not (Cached).

classes.name: common.utils.LoginRequiredNoErrorMixin
documentation:
Overwrites Django's 2.2 LoginRequiredMixin so as to not raise an error and
redirect instead.

functions.name: common.migrations.0049_anonymise_orphan_users.anonymise_orphan_users
documentation:
Users should never exist without a user-type linked to them. Anonymise all
instances of User objects without a Teacher or Student instance.

functions.name: common.migrations.0051_verify_returning_users.verify_returning_users
documentation:
Users cannot be unverified after having logged in at least once. Grab all
instances of unverified UserProfile where the User has logged in and mark it
as verified.

functions.name: common.migrations.0043_add_total_activity.add_total_activity
documentation:
This creates the only TotalActivity entry that we need to record total activity.
Initialises it with the total number of registrations at the time of the migration.

methods.name: common.migrations.0037_migrate_email_verification.Migration.forwards
documentation: Finds the users of verified Email Verification objects and sets their `is_verified` to True

methods.name: common.migrations.0037_migrate_email_verification.Migration.backwards
documentation: Finds the users of verified Email Verification objects and sets their `is_verified` to False

functions.name: common.migrations.0050_anonymise_orphan_schools.anonymise_orphan_schools
documentation:
Schools without any teachers or students should be anonymised (inactive).
Mark all active orphan schools as inactive.

methods.name: common.migrations.0041_populate_gb_counties.Migration.forwards
documentation: Populate the county field for schools in GB

functions.name: common.migrations.0030_add_maintenance_banner.add_maintenance_banner
documentation:
This creates the maintenance banner dynamic element which allows us to edit and turn on and off the maintenance
banner dynamically from the admin panel.

functions.name: common.tests.utils.user.get_superuser
documentation: Get a superuser for testing, or create one if there isn't one.

functions.name: common.tests.utils.user.create_user_directly
documentation: Create a user in the database.

functions.name: common.tests.utils.classes.create_class_directly
documentation:
Generate a class with the details given.

Args:
    teacher_email (str): The email of the teacher that will own the class.
    class_name (str, optional): The name of the class. Defaults to auto-generated name.

Returns:
    (class: Class, name: str, access_code: str): A tuple with the class model instance, name and access code.

functions.name: common.tests.utils.teacher.signup_teacher_directly
documentation:
Creates a Teacher object by using the details passed in as kwargs, or by
generating random details. Also verifies the teacher's email if preverified
is True.
\:param preverified: whether or not the teacher's email should be verified.
\:return: the teacher's email and password.

functions.name: common.tests.utils.student.create_school_student_directly
documentation:
Creates a new student in the class with the specified access code.

Args:
    access_code (str): The access code of the class the created student will be in

Returns:
    Tuple[str, str, Student]: (name, password, student)

functions.name: common.tests.utils.student.create_independent_student_directly
documentation:
Creates a Student object and makes it independent by generating random details.
Also verifies the student's email if preverified is True.
\:param preverified: whether or not the independent student's email should be
verified.
\:return: the student's username, password and the student object itself.

methods.name: common.tests.test_models.TestModels.test_indep_student_pending_class_request_on_delete
documentation:
Given a class and an independent student,
When the student makes a request to join the class and the class is deleted,
Then the student's pending class request field is set to null.

methods.name: common.tests.test_models.TestModels.test_school_admins
documentation: Test that only the admins of a school are returned by the school.admins() function.

functions.name: common.app_settings.domain
documentation: Returns the full domain depending on whether it's local, dev, staging or prod.

modules.name: common.helpers.organisation
documentation: TODO: Move to Address model once we create it

functions.name: common.helpers.data_migration_loader.load_data_from_file
documentation:
Returns a migration function that loads a json file produced by `manage.py dumpdata` into the database.
For use with migrations.RunPython

Args:
    file_name (str): The name of the file containing the data you want to load. Include `.json` at the end.
    The file must be in the fixtures directory.

functions.name: common.helpers.generators.generate_login_id
documentation:
Returns the uuid string and its hashed.
The string is used for URL, and the hashed is stored in the DB.

functions.name: common.helpers.generators.get_hashed_login_id
documentation: Returns the hash of a given string used for login url

functions.name: common.mail.add_contact
documentation: Add a new contact to Dotdigital.

functions.name: common.mail.remove_contact
documentation: Remove an existing contact from Dotdigital.

functions.name: common.mail.send_dotdigital_email
documentation:
Send a triggered email campaign using DotDigital's API.

https://developer.dotdigital.com/reference/send-transactional-email-using-a-triggered-campaign

Args:
    campaign_id: The ID of the triggered campaign, which needs to be included within the request body.
    to_addresses: The email address(es) to send to.
    cc_addresses: The CC email address or address to to send to. separate email addresses with a comma. Maximum: 100.
    bcc_addresses: The BCC email address or address to to send to. separate email addresses with a comma. Maximum: 100.
    from_address: The From address for your email. Note: The From address must already be added to your account. Otherwise, your account's default From address is used.
    personalization_values: Each personalisation value is a key-value pair; the placeholder name of the personalization value needs to be included in the request body.
    metadata: The metadata for your email. It can be either a single value or a series of values in a JSON object.
    attachments: A Base64 encoded string. All attachment types are supported. Maximum file size: 15 MB.
    region: The Dotdigital region id your account belongs to e.g. r1, r2 or r3.
    auth: The authorization header used to enable API access. If None, the value will be retrieved from the DOTDIGITAL_AUTH environment variable.
    timeout: Send timeout to avoid hanging.

Raises:
    AssertionError: If failed to send email.

classes.name: common.mail.EmailAttachment
documentation: An email attachment for a Dotdigital triggered campaign.

methods.name: common.models.SchoolModelManager.get_queryset
documentation: Filter out inactive schools by default

methods.name: common.models.TeacherModelManager.get_queryset
documentation: Filter out non active teachers by default

methods.name: common.models.SchoolTeacherInvitationModelManager.get_queryset
documentation: Filter out inactive invitations by default

methods.name: common.models.ClassModelManager.get_queryset
documentation: Filter out non active classes by default

classes.name: common.models.JoinReleaseStudent
documentation:
To keep track when a student is released to be independent student or
joins a class to be a school student.
Below are models used for data tracking and maintenance

classes.name: common.models.DailyActivity
documentation:
A model to record sets of daily activity. Currently used to record the
amount of student details download clicks, through the CSV and login
cards methods, per day.

classes.name: common.models.TotalActivity
documentation:
A model to record total activity. Meant to only have one entry which
records all total activity. An example of this is total ever registrations.

classes.name: common.models.DynamicElement
documentation:
This model is meant to allow us to quickly update some elements
dynamically on the website without having to redeploy everytime. For
example, if a maintenance banner needs to be added, we check the box in
the Django admin panel, edit the text and it'll show immediately on the
website.

functions.name: common.helpers.emails.send_verification_email
documentation:
Sends emails relating to email address verification.

On registration:
\- if the user is under 13, send a verification email addressed to the parent / guardian
\- if the user is over 13, send a regular verification email
\- if the user is a student who just got released, send a verification email explaining the situation
\- if the user is a student who has requested to sign up to the newsletter, handle their Dotmailer subscription

On email address update:
\- sends an email to the old address alerting the user that an email change request has occurred
\- sends an email to the new address requesting email address verification

```
param request

The Django form request

param user

The user object concerned with the email verification

param data

The data inputted by the user in the Django form.

param new_email

New email the user wants to associate to their account - if not provided, it means the user is
```

registering a new account
\:param age: The user's age (collected only for the purposes of this function and if the user is an independent
student)
